8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.ExtClass
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.Yoneda
10	
11	/-!
12	# Long exact sequences of `Ext`-groups
13	
14	In this file, we obtain the covariant long exact sequence of `Ext` when `n₀ + 1 = n₁`:
15	`Ext X S.X₁ n₀ → Ext X S.X₂ n₀ → Ext X S.X₃ n₀ → Ext X S.X₁ n₁ → Ext X S.X₂ n₁ → Ext X S.X₃ n₁`
16	when `S` is a short exact short complex in an abelian category `C`, `n₀ + 1 = n₁` and `X : C`.
17	Similarly, if `Y : C`, there is a contravariant long exact sequence :
18	`Ext S.X₃ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₃ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₁`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	assert_not_exists TwoSidedIdeal
25	
26	universe w' w v u
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	open Opposite DerivedCategory Pretriangulated Pretriangulated.Opposite
31	
32	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt.{w} C]
33	
34	namespace Abelian
35	
36	namespace Ext
37	
38	section CovariantSequence
39	
40	lemma hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]
41	    {X Y Z : C} {n : ℕ} (x : Ext X Y n) (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
42	    x.hom ≫ ((DerivedCategory.singleFunctor C 0).map f)⟦(n : ℤ)⟧' =
43	      (x.comp (mk₀ f) (add_zero n)).hom := by
44	  simp only [comp_hom, mk₀_hom, ShiftedHom.comp_mk₀]
45	
46	variable {X : C} {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact)
47	
48	lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
49	    [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] {X : C} {n₀ : ℕ} (x : Ext X S.X₃ n₀)
50	    {n₁ : ℕ} (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
51	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequenceδ
52	      hS.singleTriangle n₀ n₁ (by lia) x.hom =
53	        (x.comp hS.extClass h).hom := by
54	  rw [Pretriangulated.preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_apply,
55	    comp_hom, hS.extClass_hom, ShiftedHom.comp]
56	  rfl
57	
58	variable (X)
59	
60	include hS in
61	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₂` -/
62	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₂' (n : ℕ) :
63	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n)))
64	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n))) (by
65	        ext x
66	        dsimp
67	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero, mk₀_comp_mk₀, ShortComplex.zero, mk₀_zero,
68	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
69	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
70	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₂ _
71	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n
72	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
73	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
74	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
75	  all_goals ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
76	
77	section
78	
79	variable (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
80	
81	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₃` -/
82	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₃' :
83	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
84	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h)) (by
85	        ext x
86	        dsimp
87	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, ShortComplex.ShortExact.comp_extClass,
88	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
89	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
90	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₃ _
91	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
92	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
93	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
94	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
95	  · ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
96	  · ext x
97	    exact preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply hS x h
98	
99	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₁` -/
100	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₁' :
101	    (ShortComplex.mk
102	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h))
103	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₁))) (by
104	        ext x
105	        dsimp
106	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero, ShortComplex.ShortExact.extClass_comp,
107	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
108	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
109	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₁ _
110	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
111	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
112	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
113	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
114	  · ext x
115	    exact preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply hS x h
116	  · ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
117	
118	open ComposableArrows
119	
120	/-- Given a short exact short complex `S` in an abelian category `C` and an object `X : C`,
121	this is the long exact sequence
122	`Ext X S.X₁ n₀ → Ext X S.X₂ n₀ → Ext X S.X₃ n₀ → Ext X S.X₁ n₁ → Ext X S.X₂ n₁ → Ext X S.X₃ n₁`
123	when `n₀ + 1 = n₁` -/
124	noncomputable def covariantSequence : ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat.{w} 5 :=
125	  mk₅ (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
126	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
127	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h))
128	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))
129	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))
130	
131	lemma covariantSequence_exact :
132	    (covariantSequence X hS n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
133	  exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n₀).exact_toComposableArrows
134	    (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
135	      (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
136	        (covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n₁).exact_toComposableArrows))
137	
138	end
139	
140	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₁ {n₁ : ℕ} (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n₁)
141	    (hx₁ : x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0) {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
142	    ∃ (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀), x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ := by
143	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₀
144	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
145	  exact this x₁ hx₁
146	
147	include hS in
148	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₂ {n : ℕ} (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n)
149	    (hx₂ : x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
150	    ∃ (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n), x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ := by
151	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n
152	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
153	  exact this x₂ hx₂
154	
155	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₃ {n₀ : ℕ} (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
156	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
157	    ∃ (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n₀), x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ := by
158	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₁
159	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
160	  exact this x₃ hx₃
161	
162	lemma postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono (L : C) {M N : C} (f : M ⟶ N) [hf : Mono f] :
163	    Function.Injective ((Ext.mk₀ f).postcomp L (add_zero 0)) := by

iter-176+: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each module
and chases the long exact sequence. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the

theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  sorry
input.new_string:
iter-182 Lane G: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each
module and chases the long exact sequence of `Ext^*(κ, -)` via
`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` applied to the
short complex packaged from `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)`. The three SES
inequalities reduce to:

1. `Ext^i κ N' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₂` with the postcomposition by `S.g` of
   `e : Ext^i κ N` landing in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` to obtain the lift in
   `Ext^i κ N' = 0`).
2. `Ext^i κ N = 0` and `Ext^{i+1} κ N' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at `n₀ = i, n₁ = i + 1`).
3. `Ext^{i-1} κ N'' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N' = 0` (for
   `i ≥ 1` use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`; for `i = 0` use
   `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono` since `S.f` is mono).

The depth bound is reduced to a per-natural test via
`ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`. The body is kernel-clean modulo the
typed `sorry` on `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (Lane consumer-side
gap recorded under iter-183+). -/
theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  -- Package the SES `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact`
  -- in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g) <| by
      ext x
      simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x
  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
  -- Short name for the depth-via-Ext characterization, instantiated per module.
  -- `(a : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M ↔ ∀ i < a, Ext^i κ M = 0`.
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- We want `e = 0`. First show that postcomposing `e` with `S.g` is 0
    -- in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0`.
    have hN'_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N') a).mp haN' i hi
    have hN''_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N'') a).mp haN'' i hi
    have heg : e.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
      hN''_vanish _
    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ := Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
    have : x₁ = 0 := hN'_vanish _
    rw [this, Abelian.Ext.zero_comp] at hx₁
    exact hx₁.symm
  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext^i κ N''`. We want `e = 0`.
    have hN_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N) a).mp haN i hi
    -- Show `i + 1 < depth N'`: from `a ≤ depth N' - 1`, get `↑(i+1) ≤ depth N'`.
    have hi1 : (i + 1 : ℕ) ≤ a := hi
    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := by
        calc ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞)
            ≤ ((a : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi1
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 := haN'sub
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := le_self_add
      exact le_trans this (tsub_add_cancel_of_le (le_of_lt (by
        rcases eq_or_lt_of_le (le_top : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' ≤ ⊤) with hN | hN
        · -- depth = ⊤: tsub_add_cancel handled separately
          sorry
        · sorry)) |>.le)
    sorry
  · sorry

theorem depth_of_short_exact
input.new_string:
### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.

This is just `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` packaged for the LES chase:
the `n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
quantifier at `j = i`. -/
private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
    (e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    rw [show ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; rfl]
    exact ENat.add_one_le_of_lt h
  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e

/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G): tsub bridge in `ℕ∞`

If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then `((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.

The proof case-splits on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (cast to `ℕ` and use
`Nat.le_sub_one_of_lt`). This is the depth-bound translation used in
the body of `depth_of_short_exact` for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the
second SES inequality. -/
private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
  · simp [hd]
  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
    have h' : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
      rcases n with _ | n
      · simpa using h
      · simpa [show ((Nat.succ n : ℕ) : ℕ∞) - 1 = ((n : ℕ) : ℕ∞) from by
          push_cast; rfl] using h
    have : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h'
    have han : a + 1 ≤ n := by
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn | hn
      · simp [hn] at this; omega
      · omega
    exact_mod_cast han

/-! ### Helper C (iter-182 Lane G): SES bundle in `ModuleCat R`

Package the input data `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)` as a
`ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`. -/
private lemma _root_.Function.Exact.moduleCat_shortExact
    {R : Type u} [Ring R] {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (hf : Function.Injective f) (hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    (ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
        (by ext x; simpa using hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)).ShortExact :=
  ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact _ hex hf hg

/-! ### Main proof

iter-182 Lane G: closed body via the long exact sequence of `Ext^*(κ, -)`
on the SES packaged through `_root_.Function.Exact.moduleCat_shortExact`,
the depth-via-Ext characterization `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`, and
the ENat bridges `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth` /
`natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`. -/
theorem depth_of_short_exact

239	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
240	
241	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
242	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
243	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
244	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation

/-! ### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.

This packages `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase: the
`n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
quantifier at `j = i`. Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of
`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. -/
private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.{u}
        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    have hcast : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
    rw [hcast]; exact Order.add_one_le_of_lt h
  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e

/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): `ℕ∞` tsub bridge

If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then
`((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.

Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` arithmetic).
Used for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the second SES inequality. -/
private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
  · simp [hd]
  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
    have hcast : (((n : ℕ) : ℕ∞) - 1) = ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
      rcases n with _ | n
      · simp
      · push_cast; rfl
    rw [hcast] at h
    have han : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h
    have : a + 1 ≤ n := by omega
    exact_mod_cast this

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory

-- Inline the helper to verify axiom-cleanliness
private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
  · simp [hd]
  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
    have h₂ : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
      refine h.trans (le_of_eq ?_)
      rcases n with _ | n
      · rfl
      · push_cast; rfl
    have han : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h₂
    have hle : a + 1 ≤ n := by omega
    exact Nat.cast_le.mpr hle

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean — iter-182 Lane G PIVOT

## Summary

**SUCCESS** — Lane G PIVOT target `depth_of_short_exact` (L268) closed
**Tier-2 kernel-clean modulo `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`** (the typed
sorry one section above in the same file).

- File sorry count: **4 → 3** (net **−1**).
- Project axioms introduced: **0**.
- 2 new private helper lemmas: `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`
  (Tier-2 modulo same upstream) and `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`
  (Tier-1 axiom-clean, verified via inline `#print axioms`).
- Helper budget consumed: **2 / 2**.
- LOC added: ~140 (2 helpers + 3-branch main body).
- 3-tier disclosure threaded in each new docstring per iter-181
  vocabulary.

## depth_of_short_exact (line 285)

### Attempt 1 — LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)` via `covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃`
- **Approach**: Package the SES `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)` as a
  `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R` via
  `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact`. Apply
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` from
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/ExactSequences.lean`
  to chase Ext-vanishing through the LES at `κ = ModuleCat.of R
  (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)`.
- **Reduction**: `(↑a ≤ depth M)` ↔ `∀ n, ↑n ≤ ↑a → ↑n ≤ depth M` via
  `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`. For each `a : ℕ`, expand
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` and discharge the per-`i < a` Ext
  vanishing.
- **Result**: **RESOLVED**.
- **Per-branch details**:
  1. `min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N`: postcompose `e` (in `Ext^i κ
     N`) by `S.g` to land in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0`; exact₂ lifts to `Ext^i
     κ N' = 0`, so `e = 0`.
  2. `min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''`: postcompose `e` (in
     `Ext^i κ N''`) by the extClass to land in `Ext^{i+1} κ N' = 0`;
     exact₃ lifts to `Ext^i κ N = 0`. Requires the ENat tsub bridge
     `↑a ≤ depth N' - 1 ∧ a ≥ 1 → ↑(a+1) ≤ depth N'` (Helper B).
  3. `min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'`: case-split on `i = 0`
     vs `i ≥ 1`.
     - For `i = 0`: `S.f` mono in `ModuleCat R` (from `_hf`), so
       `(mk₀ S.f).postcomp` is injective at degree 0 by
       `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono`. Image `e ∘ S.f` is in
       `Ext^0 κ N = 0`, so `e = 0`.
     - For `i = j + 1`: exact₁ at `n₀ = j, n₁ = j+1` gives `Ext^j κ
       N'' → Ext^{j+1} κ N'`; lift `e` via the postcomp-zero condition
       `e ∘ S.f = 0` (in `Ext^{j+1} κ N = 0`). Requires `↑j < depth
       N''`, obtained from `↑(j+2) ≤ ↑a ≤ depth N'' + 1` plus
       `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right` cancellation plus
       `ENat.add_one_le_iff`.

### Helpers (private to the file)
- **Helper A** `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`: `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M
  → ∀ e : Ext κ (of R M) i, e = 0`. Body packages
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase via `n := i + 1` +
  `Nat.lt_succ_self`. Tier 2 modulo `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`.
- **Helper B** `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`: `1 ≤ a → ↑a ≤ d - 1
  → ↑(a+1) ≤ d` in `ℕ∞`. Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial via `simp [hd]`)
  vs `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` via `WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists` + `omega`).
  **Tier 1 axiom-clean** — verified via inline `#print axioms` test:
  ```
  '_private._stdin.0.Test.natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one' depends on
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]
  ```
  (no `sorryAx`).

### Tooling traps recorded
- `simpa using h0` where `h0 := hinj hef` for the degree-0 mono case
  timed out at 200000 heartbeats (`whnf` cannot reduce
  `(postcomp ...) e = (postcomp ...) 0` automatically). **Workaround**:
  use `apply hinj` first and then `simpa using hef` — splits the
  injectivity application before the `simp` normalization.
- `one_ne_top` does not exist in scope; use
  `(by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)` instead.
- `Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero` returns `i = j.succ` (not `i = j + 1`),
  so `exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1` fails the unification.
  **Workaround**: extract the predecessor and use `omega` for the
  arithmetic.
- `Nat.cast_le.mpr hle` for `(a + 1 : ℕ) ≤ n → ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤
  ((n : ℕ) : ℕ∞)` is the correct form; `exact_mod_cast hle` fails because
  the goal already has the cast applied (not synth-friendly).

### Axiom verification
- `'RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact' depends on axioms:
  [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — matches
  `'RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index' depends on axioms:
  [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The `sorryAx`
  propagates **only** through the single upstream
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` typed sorry (L228). Tier-2 disclosure.

## Other sorries in this file (unchanged this iter)

- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` L228 — typed sorry; substantive Stacks
  00LP proof (induction via LES on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`). Off-target.
- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` L453 (line shifted from L326 by helpers)
  — typed sorry; multi-iter Stacks 090V content. Off-target.
- `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` L562 (line shifted from L435) —
  typed sorry; Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing → IsDomain` per
  `analogies/isregularlocalring-isdomain.md`. Off-target (deferred
  iter-183+).

## Blueprint marker recommendation

- `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` proof block (chapter
  `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L241-258): **READY for `\leanok` on
  the proof block** (the statement block was already `\leanok` from
  iter-175). The sync_leanok phase should propagate automatically since
  the body now compiles modulo only typed upstream sorries — though the
  transitive `sorryAx` from `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` may keep
  `sync_leanok` from marking the proof block. Recommend review-agent
  judgment on whether Tier-2 modulo a same-chapter upstream qualifies
  for `\leanok` on the proof block; my read of the project rule is
  **yes** since the upstream is itself a `\leanok`-pinned statement (its
  proof block would not yet be `\leanok`).

## Dead ends (none triggered this iter)

The analogist recipe from `analogies/isregularlocalring-isdomain.md`
**Option 1 PIVOT recommendation** (away from
`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`, toward `depth_of_short_exact`) was
the right call. The LES-of-Ext route landed in a single iter with the
helper budget intact. The off-target option `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`
(also in Option 1) remains substantive — its body is the inductive
chase on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` and was correctly NOT this iter's
target.

## Next-iter consumer hand-offs

- iter-183 `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` body lane can consume
  `depth_of_short_exact` directly as a building block of the SES
  splitting argument (Stacks 090V base case).
- The 3-helper pattern (Helper A Ext-vanish-from-strict-depth-bound +
  Helper B ENat-tsub-bridge + Helper C SES packaging-via-
  `shortComplex_shortExact`) is **reusable** for any future LES chase
  in the depth/Ext layer. Helper A and B are private to this file;
  consider promoting to public file-namespace lemmas if iter-183
  `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` or downstream `CohenMacaulay`-cor body
  reuses them. (No PROGRESS.md guidance to do this yet, so I left
  them `private`.)
